game_name: Shadow Dancer: The Secret of Shinobi

design_philosophy.core_loop: Navigate side-scrolling stages, rescue hostages, command dog companion for tactical combat, defeat bosses to progress
design_philosophy.primary_tension: One-hit-point-per-life survival against enemy placement variety, balanced by generous hidden bonus lives and dog companion mechanics
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Urban ninja warrior with loyal canine partner, emerging from hiding to dismantle an evil cult occupying a ruined city
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: The dog companion is not cosmetic—it's a core mechanic requiring active meter management and positioning awareness. Hostage rescue replaces arcade's bomb retrieval, incentivizing non-lethal exploration. Three selectable difficulty tiers with variable enemy placement allow players to customize challenge while maintaining one-hit death.

difficulty.lives_system: One-hit-point-per-life system retained from arcade version. Numerous hidden bonus lives scattered throughout stages. Hostage rescue mechanics (male hostages grant bonus points and occasional extra lives) provide life economy management.
difficulty.continues_system: Number of continues varies by selected difficulty tier (three difficulty settings available)
difficulty.checkpointing: Stage-based checkpointing. Five total stages with first four containing three areas each (third area is boss). Final stage is series of five rooms leading to final boss.
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: High agency via three selectable difficulty modes affecting enemy placement and continue availability. Optional shuriken restriction available (disabled except for boss battles and bonus stages). Bonus point system with hidden conditional bonuses rewards mastery and risk-taking.

progression.upgrade_system: Female hostage rescue grants temporary protagonist attack enhancement lasting until next stage or death. Dog companion meter acts as variable resource requiring management and positioning strategy.
progression.unlock_mechanism: Stage progression unlocks subsequent levels. Bonus stage appears between rounds, offering life rewards based on performance (shooting down 50 enemy ninjas; perfect run grants three lives).
replayability_hooks: Hidden bonus conditions reward stage/boss completion under specific circumstances
Three difficulty tiers encourage replay with different enemy placements
Shuriken restriction toggle adds strategic variation to approach
Bonus stage performance variation (1-3 lives based on enemy hit count)
Hostage rescue mechanics incentivize exploration for enhancements

world_building_techniques: Environmental storytelling: New York City transformed into ruins by Union Lizard cult
Character identity ambiguity: Protagonist identity varies between Japanese manual (Hayate, son of Joe Musashi) and English manual (Joe Musashi emerging from retirement)
Objective framing: Rescuing hostages from cult imprisonment establishes narrative stakes
Enemy behavior detail: Environmental storytelling through enemy actions (enemies duck behind boxes to reload weapons)
Setting design: Gritty urban landscape establishes tone and faction presence

boss_design.philosophy: Boss encounters serve as stage conclusions with improved design noted as superior to arcade original. Reviewers specifically praised 'greatly improved difficulty level' of bosses compared to source material.
notable_bosses: Sauros (final boss at end of fifth stage's five-room gauntlet)

faction_indicators: Union Lizard: evil ninja cult worshipping giant reptilian demon (Sauros)
Cult occupies and ruins New York City in 1997
danger_signals: Cult's reptilian demon worship suggests ideological extremism
environmental_storytelling: New York City transformed from metropolis to ruins signals societal collapse
Enemy positioning and placement patterns indicate cult military organization
Hostage locations reveal cult's control infrastructure
Enemy behavior (tactical reloading behind cover) demonstrates organized faction vs. ad-hoc opposition
